title: clusia crenata
intro: Clusia crenata is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its unique, elliptical leaves and small, white flowers. Clusia crenata has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Clusia crenata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Clusia crenata is typically prepared as a tea or infusion by boiling its leaves in water, and it can also be used as a topical application for skin irritations and wounds.
